facts.text: Noroton Heights was home to the first U.S. facility for disabled war veterans and soldiers’ orphans, built in 1864 by Benjamin Fitch.
The community sits on Connecticut’s Gold Coast, just east of Stamford and about 35 miles northeast of Midtown Manhattan.
Noroton Heights borders Stamford to the west, Noroton to the south, and downtown Darien to the northeast.
Darien, the town that contains Noroton Heights, was incorporated in 1820 after separating from Stamford and becoming Middlesex Parish in 1737.
The New Haven Railroad’s first scheduled line arrived in Darien in 1848, shifting commerce from the harbor to the railroad station at the Post Road crossing.
The original settlers of the area took title to the land in 1640 when the New Haven Colony purchased a tract from the Siwanoy Indians where the Rippowam River meets Long Island Sound.
